name: American Debt Resources
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing and credit counseling agency established in 2001, offering certified guidance for homebuyers, foreclosure prevention, and debt management in New York.
description_long:
American Debt Resources, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization founded in 2001 by seasoned professionals in the credit counseling industry. The organization was established with a mission to provide education and counseling services that improve financial understanding and empower consumers to make better financial decisions. Over more than two decades, the organization has expanded its service offerings to respond to evolving economic needs while maintaining its core commitment to consumer education and financial empowerment.

The organization provides comprehensive counseling services including pre-purchase and post-purchase housing counseling, foreclosure prevention assistance, reverse mortgage education, debt management planning, and bankruptcy counseling. All counselors are trained and certified to deliver personalized guidance tailored to each client's unique financial situation. The organization emphasizes understanding individual circumstances and developing realistic, achievable strategies focused on long-term financial stability and debt-free living.

American Debt Resources distinguishes itself through its accreditation under the British Standard Institution's ISO 9001:2008 code of standards, demonstrating commitment to quality management and professional service delivery. The organization has developed strong partnerships with leading banks and financial institutions, which it leverages to help clients secure more effective solutions. Since 2001, the organization reports having guided thousands of clients toward financial stability, with counselors taking time to build relationships and understand each client's specific circumstances.

The organization operates as a legitimate non-profit with established credentials and industry partnerships. However, the website provides limited detail on success rates, specific outcomes, or client testimonials. Geographic service appears focused on New York, which may limit accessibility for consumers in other states. As a free-help provider, the organization relies on certification and accreditation rather than profit incentives, which generally aligns with consumer protection interests.
pros: 501(c)(3) non-profit status ensures services are mission-driven rather than profit-motivated
ISO 9001:2008 accreditation through BSI demonstrates adherence to international quality standards
All counselors are trained and certified housing and credit counselors
Established organization in operation since 2001 with thousands of clients served
Offers foreclosure prevention counseling with direct partnerships to leading banks
Comprehensive services including reverse mortgage education for seniors
Personalized counseling approach that assesses individual financial situations rather than one-size-fits-all solutions
cons: Website provides no specific data on success rates, default rates, or client outcomes
Geographic service appears limited to New York with no mention of national reach
No client testimonials or case studies provided on the website
Limited information on pricing structure or cost of services (if any fees apply)
No online intake or assessment tool visible; requires contacting organization directly
best_for: Homebuyers seeking pre-purchase education and guidance in New York
Homeowners facing foreclosure threats seeking prevention assistance and bank negotiation
Seniors exploring reverse mortgage options who want certified counseling before committing
diagnosis: Best for New York residents facing specific housing challenges (foreclosure, reverse mortgages, homebuying) or general debt management who want certified non-profit guidance. Primary caveat is limited geographic service area and lack of published outcome data to verify effectiveness.
services: Bank partnership negotiations for foreclosure solutions
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
